\"{{name}}\" already exists. Click Confi: "{{name}}" already exists. Click Confirm to overwrite.
* Drag & drop rows from the # column to: * Drag & drop rows from the # column to re-order
*You will need to manually move any exis: *You will need to manually move any existing cached files to their new location.
Are you sure you want to delete {{n}} pl: Are you sure you want to delete {{n}} playlist(s)?
Are you sure you want to delete this pla: Are you sure you want to delete this playlist?
Are you sure you want to reset your sett: Are you sure you want to reset your settings to default?
Click a shortcut to change it, then pres: Click a shortcut to change it, then press the new key combination. Press Escape to cancel.
Config for integration with external pro: Config for integration with external programs.
Customize the columns, row height, and f: Customize the columns, row height, and font size for each list view (Songs, Albums, Playlists, etc.). Select a view tab below, then drag columns to reorder them or toggle them on and off.
Default to Album List on Artist Page: Default to Album List on Artist Page
Do you want to restart the application n: Do you want to restart the application now?
Don't know where to start? Apply a prese: Don't know where to start? Apply a preset and tweak from there.
Enable or disable global media hotkeys (: Enable or disable global media hotkeys (play/pause, next, previous, stop, etc). For macOS, you will need to add Sonixd as a <2>trusted accessibility client.</2> If you plan to bind media keys in the Keyboard Shortcuts tab, it is advised to disable this option to avoid conflicts.
Enable or disable the volume fade used b: Enable or disable the volume fade used by the crossfading players. If disabled, the fading in track will start at full volume.
Enable or disable the Windows System Med: Enable or disable the Windows System Media Transport Controls (play/pause, next, previous, stop). This will show the Windows Media Popup (Windows 10 only) when pressing a media key. This feauture will override the Global Media Hotkeys option. If you plan to bind media keys in the Keyboard Shortcuts tab, it is advised to disable this option to avoid conflicts.
Enables or disables automatic updates. W: Enables or disables automatic updates. When a new version is detected, it will automatically be downloaded and installed.
Enabling this will open the Artist page: Enabling this will open the Artist page to their list of albums instead of the main page.
Failed to import settings: invalid file.
Filter out tracks based on regex string(: Filter out tracks based on regex string(s) by their title when adding to the queue. Adding by double-clicking a track will ignore all filters for that one track.
Highlights the list view row when hoveri: Highlights the list view row when hovering it with the mouse.
If local, scrobbles the currently playin: If local, scrobbles the currently playing song to local .txt files. If web, scrobbles the currently playing song to Tuna plugin's webserver.
If your audio files are not playing prop: If your audio files are not playing properly or are not in a supported web streaming format, you will need to enable this (requires app restart).
Integrates with Discord's rich presence: Integrates with Discord's rich presence to display the currently playing song as your status.
Name: Name
No songs found, adjust your filters: No songs found, adjust your filters
Note: These settings may not function co: Note: These settings may not function correctly depending on your desktop environment.
Page Sonixd will display on start.
Path: {{newCachePath}} not found. Enter: Path: {{newCachePath}} not found. Enter a valid path.
Percentage of a track that must be playe: Percentage of a track that must be played before it is scrobbled. A track is also scrobbled after 4 minutes regardless of this setting.
Restore settings from a previously expor: Restore settings from a previously exported file. The app will reload after importing.
Retains the size and position of the app: Retains the size and position of the application window. Size is only saved when the program is exited properly!
Save (WARNING: Closing the application w: Save (WARNING: Closing the application while saving may result in data loss)
Save your current settings to a JSON fil: Save your current settings to a JSON file. Server credentials are not included.
Saves your entire play queue and restore: Saves your entire play queue and restores it on next launch, including your position in the queue.
Select which pages to apply media folder: Select which pages to apply media folder filtering to:
Selected audio device is no longer avail: Selected audio device is no longer available. Using system default.
Send player updates to your server. This: Send player updates to your server. This is required by servers such as Jellyfin and Navidrome to track play counts and use external services such as Last.fm.
Sets a dynamic background based on the c: Sets a dynamic background based on the currently playing song.
Sets the parent media folder your audio: Sets the parent media folder your audio files are located in. Leaving this blank will use all media folders.
Show a system notification whenever the: Show a system notification whenever the song changes.
Songs are cached only when playback for: Songs are cached only when playback for the track fully completes and ends. Skipping to the next or previous track after only partially completing the track will not begin the caching process.
The alignment of cards in the grid view: The alignment of cards in the grid view layout.
The application theme. Want to create yo: The application theme. Want to create your own themes? Check out the documentation <2>here</2>.
The audio device for Sonixd. Leaving thi: The audio device for Sonixd. Leaving this blank will use the system default.
The client/application Id of the Sonixd: The client/application Id of the Sonixd discord application. To use your own, create one on the <2>developer application portal</2>. The large icon uses the name "icon". Default is 923372440934055968.
The default album page sort selection on: The default album page sort selection on application startup.
The default height to use when Retain Wi: The default height to use when Retain Window Size is disabled. Default: 728
The default song page sort selection on: The default song page sort selection on application startup.
The default width to use when Retain Win: The default width to use when Retain Window Size is disabled. Default: 1024
The fade calculation to use when crossfa: The fade calculation to use when crossfading between two tracks.
The full path to the directory where son: The full path to the directory where song metadata will be created.
The full URL to the Tuna webserver.
The gap in pixels (px) of the grid view: The gap in pixels (px) of the grid view layout.
The height in pixels (px) of each row in: The height in pixels (px) of each row in the list view.
The number in milliseconds (ms) between: The number in milliseconds (ms) between each poll when metadata is sent.
The number in milliseconds between each: The number in milliseconds between each poll when music is playing. This is used in the calculation for crossfading and gapless playback. Recommended value for gapless playback is between 10 and 20.
The number in seconds before starting th: The number in seconds before starting the crossfade to the next track. Setting this to 0 will enable gapless playback.
The number in seconds the player will sk: The number in seconds the player will skip backwards when clicking the seek backward button.
The number in seconds the player will skip forwards when clicking the seek forward button.
The number of items that will be retriev: The number of items that will be retrieved per page. Setting this to 0 will disable pagination.
The width and height in pixels (px) of e: The width and height in pixels (px) of each grid view card.
Title: Title
Uses the song image returned by your ser: Uses the song image returned by your server (only works if your server is publically accessible).
WARNING: This will reload the applicatio: WARNING: This will reload the application
When enabled, songs added via \"Play Nex: When enabled, songs added via "Play Next" are queued in the order they were added. When disabled, each "Play Next" inserts at the top of the queue.
When enabled, the 6 playback shortcuts (: When enabled, the 6 playback shortcuts (play/pause, next, previous, volume, mute) work even when the app is not focused. Note: these shortcuts are captured at the OS level and will override the same key combinations in other apps.
When enabled, the previous button always: When enabled, the previous button always goes to the previous song. When disabled, it restarts the current song if you are more than 5 seconds in.
Which cache would you like to clear?: Which cache would you like to clear?
